rulings.Case Title: MARIEL HERNANDEZ VS. MARILU ANGELES et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, FEBRUARY 6, 2008 LINE 3. DEFENDANTS MARILU ANGELES, METZI HERNANDEZ AND EMELIN HERNANDEZ'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND FOR THE REASONS STATED IN THE COURT MINUTES OF AUGUST 2, 2007. BRIEFLY, DEFENDANTS DISCHARGED THEIR DUTY TO PLAINTIFF UNDER RUTTENBERG V RUTTENBERG (1997) 53 CAL APP 4TH 801. THIS IS DEMONSTRATED BY PLAINTIFF'S ACTIVE PARTICIPATION IN THE UNDERLYING LITIGATION. THE DELAY IN SERVING PLAINTIFF DID NOT IMPEDE HER ABILITY TO ASSERT HER RIGHTS AS TO THE ECONOMIC BENEFITS FOR THE HARM CAUSED TO HER BY THE LOSS OF HER FATHER. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: MARIEL HERNANDEZ VS. MARILU ANGELES et al

rulings.Case Title: ETHEL GRANT VS. PRIME MORTGAGE FUNDING, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, FEBRUARY 6, 2008 LINE 6. DEFENDANT TICOR TITLE COMPANY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT: Ticor's demurrer to each of the causes of action is sustained with 30 days leave to amend. The allegations as to Ticor do not reflect conduct apart from acting as an escrow agent. The allegations regarding the notarized documents are contradicted by the documents themselves; the failure to respond to plaintiff's request in August 2007 is not actionable for the wrong had been perpetrated long before; and the failure to follow the mortgage company's instructions may give that company a claim, but does not, standing alone, give plaintiff a claim. =(302/PJM/X-MS)

rulings.Case Title: ETHEL GRANT VS. PRIME MORTGAGE FUNDING, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Joinder In Demurrer Of Itcor Title Co To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, FEBRUARY 6, 2008 LINE 6. DEFENDANT BROOKSAMERICA MORTGAGE CORPORATION'S Joinder In Demurrer Of Itcor Title Co. To FIRST Amended Complaint: The demurrer of Brooksamerica, by way of joinder, is overruled for this defendant asks the court to treat the lender in same fashion as the escrow holder without any explanation as to why that is so and where the complaint, paragraph 19, is far more specific as to this defendant's conduct. =(302/PJM/X-MS)

rulings.Case Title: EXIGEN, LTD. VS. DIMITRI VAYNBLAT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Compel Arbitration & Mtn To Stay Litigation On Xcomplaint; Appendix Of Ono-California Authorities
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, FEBRUARY 6, 2008 LINE 8. PLAINTIFFS EXIGEN, LTD. AND GREGORY SHENKMAN'S MOTION To Compel Arbitration AND MOTION To Stay Litigation On CROSS-complaint IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 26, 2008. THE COURT REQUIRES THAT THE MOVING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE THE COURT WITH ANOTHER SET OF COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR PAPERS. WHEN SUBMITTING THE DUPLICATE COPIES, PLEASE PROVIDE A COVER LETTER TO THE COURT REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE SO THAT THE COURTESY COPIES WON'T BE MISPLACED WHEN RECEIVED. =(302/PJM)
